title: L06 · Stem-Changers & Common Irregulars
objective: Master the three stem-change patterns (e→ie, o→ue, e→i) and the 10 most useful fully irregular verbs. After this lesson, you'll cover 95% of present-tense verbs you'll meet.
strategy: Stem-changers follow a SHAPE: change happens in yo/tú/él/ellos forms only — NEVER in nosotros and vosotros. Picture a 'boot' or 'shoe' around the 4 forms that change, with the 2 plural-but-not-they-all forms outside. Section 5 covers the irregulars to memorise.

sections.title: The 'boot' pattern
sections.note: Critical visual: only 4 forms stem-change. The 'we' and 'you all' forms (nosotros, vosotros) keep the original stem.

blocks.text: Stem change in: yo, tú, él/ella/usted, ellos. NOT in: nosotros, vosotros.
blocks.audioText: pienso, piensas, piensa, pensamos, pensáis, piensan
blocks.rule: Think of a boot/shoe shape on the conjugation table. If you write the 6 forms in 2 columns, the changed forms make a 'boot' around the corners + middle.

examples.translation: I think, we think, they think — note 'pensamos' keeps the e

sections.title: e → ie stem change
sections.note: The most common pattern. The 'e' in the stem becomes 'ie' in the 4 boot forms.

blocks.rule: e → ie in yo/tú/él/ellos. Notice: nosotros stays 'pensamos' (no change).

examples.translation: I think you're right. / Je pense que tu as raison.
What do you think? / Qu'en penses-tu?

blocks.translation: to want, to love / vouloir, aimer
blocks.rule: e → ie. Crucial verb. 'Querer + person' = to love (a person). 'Querer + thing' = to want.

examples.translation: I want a coffee. / Je veux un café.
I love you. (used between family, partners, close friends) / Je t'aime.
What do you want to eat? / Qu'est-ce que tu veux manger?

blocks.rule: e → ie. Often followed by 'a + infinitive': 'empiezo a estudiar' = I start to study.

examples.translation: I start at 9. / Je commence à 9h.

examples.text: El concierto empieza a las ocho.
examples.audioText: El concierto empieza a las ocho.
examples.translation: The concert starts at 8. / Le concert commence à 8h.
I don't understand. / Je ne comprends pas. — your survival sentence!

sections.title: o → ue stem change
sections.note: Second most common. The 'o' in the stem becomes 'ue' in the boot forms.

blocks.text: poder — to be able to, can
blocks.rule: o → ue. The most-used modal verb in Spanish. Followed by an infinitive.

examples.translation: Can you help me? / Peux-tu m'aider?
I can't go tomorrow. / Je ne peux pas y aller demain.
I sleep eight hours. / Je dors huit heures.
The baby is sleeping. / Le bébé dort.

blocks.text: volver — to return, to come back

examples.text: Vuelvo a casa a las seis.
examples.audioText: Vuelvo a casa a las seis.
examples.translation: I come home at 6. / Je rentre à la maison à 6h.
I can't find my keys. / Je ne trouve pas mes clés.

sections.title: e → i stem change (-IR verbs only)
sections.note: Less common — only for some -ir verbs. The 'e' becomes 'i' (not 'ie').

blocks.text: pedir — to ask for, to order
blocks.translation: to ask for, to order / demander, commander
blocks.rule: e → i. Used when ASKING FOR something or ORDERING at a restaurant. 'Pedir' = ask for a thing. 'Preguntar' = ask a question. These are different verbs!

examples.translation: I'll order a coffee, please. / Je commande un café, s'il vous plaît.
I'm asking him a favour. / Je lui demande un service.
Can you repeat, please? / Peux-tu répéter, s'il te plaît?

sections.title: The 10 fully irregular verbs you'll use daily
sections.note: These don't follow a pattern. Memorise them as units.

blocks.rule: Completely irregular. Crucial because of the 'ir + a + infinitive' = 'going to do X' future construction.

examples.translation: I go / I'm going to Madrid. / Je vais à Madrid.
I'm going to study tonight. / Je vais étudier ce soir. — same structure as French aller + inf
Let's go! / Allons-y!

blocks.rule: Yo form irregular: 'hago'. Rest is regular -er.

examples.translation: What are you doing? / Qu'est-ce que tu fais?
I'm making dinner. / Je prépare le dîner.

blocks.rule: Yo irregular (digo) + e→i pattern. Two irregularities at once.

examples.translation: I'm telling the truth. / Je dis la vérité.

blocks.rule: Yo form: 'sé' (one letter, with accent). For knowing FACTS or HOW TO do something. 'Sé nadar' = I know how to swim.

examples.translation: I know how to speak English. / Je sais parler anglais.
I don't know. / Je ne sais pas.

blocks.rule: Yo form: 'conozco' (with extra z). For knowing PEOPLE or PLACES (being familiar with them). The distinction saber/conocer mirrors French savoir/connaître.

examples.translation: I know Maria. / Je connais Maria.
We know Barcelona. (= we've been there) / Nous connaissons Barcelone.
I'm watching a film. / Je regarde un film.

blocks.rule: Yo form: 'doy' (parallel to 'voy' and 'estoy'). Rest is like -ar.

examples.translation: I'm giving you a gift. / Je te donne un cadeau.
I'm setting the table. / Je mets la table.

blocks.text: salir — to leave, to go out

examples.text: Salgo con mis amigos esta noche.
examples.audioText: Salgo con mis amigos esta noche.
examples.translation: I'm going out with friends tonight. / Je sors avec mes amis ce soir.
Are you coming with me? / Tu viens avec moi?
